ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the director of the movie */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget for the movie */,
  "popularity" REAL /* Popularity score of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the movie */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the movie in minutes */,
  "vote_average" REAL /* Average user rating for the movie */,
  "vote_count" INTEGER /* Number of user ratings for the movie */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* A short, catchy phrase that summarizes the movie */,
  "poster_path" TEXT /* The path to the movie's poster image */,
  "backdrop_path" TEXT /* The path to the movie's backdrop image */,
  "status" TEXT /* The current status of the movie */,
  "production_cost" REAL /* The total production cost */,
  "marketing_cost" REAL /* The cost spent on marketing and promotion */,
  "distributor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the distributor of the movie */,
  "distributor_fee" REAL /* The fee paid to the distributor */,
  "is_sequel" INTEGER /* Indicates if the movie is a sequel */,
  "is_remake" INTEGER /* Indicates if the movie is a remake */,
  "original_movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the original movie if it is a sequel or remake */,
  "is_series" INTEGER /* Indicates if the movie is part of a series */,
  "series_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the series if the movie is part of a series */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_series_id FOREIGN KEY ("series_id") REFERENCES series ("series_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_original_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("original_movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_distributor_id FOREIGN KEY ("distributor_id") REFERENCES distributors ("distributor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* The birth date of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* The nationality of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* A brief biography of the director */,
  "image_path" TEXT /* The path to the director's image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword_name" TEXT /* Name of the keyword */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_keywords" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "founded_date" TEXT /* The date the company was founded */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* The location of the company's headquarters */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the company */,
  "logo_path" TEXT /* The path to the company's logo */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_production_companies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the production country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* The ISO code of the country */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_production_countries" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the production country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cast" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cast member */,
  "cast_name" TEXT /* Name of the cast member */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* The birth date of the cast member */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* The nationality of the cast member */,
  "biography" TEXT /* A brief biography of the cast member */,
  "image_path" TEXT /* The path to the cast member's image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_cast" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cast member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "cast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* The ISO code of the language */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_languages" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "distributors" (
  "distributor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distributor */,
  "distributor_name" TEXT /* Name of the distributor */,
  "founded_date" TEXT /* The date the distributor was founded */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* The location of the distributor's headquarters */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the distributor */,
  "logo_path" TEXT /* The path to the distributor's logo */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distributor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "series" (
  "series_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each series */,
  "series_name" TEXT /* Name of the series */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the series */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("series_id")
)
